title: ferray-fft: fftshift/ifftshift allocate and copy the entire array
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

fftshift() and ifftshift() in shift.rs do a full collect of the input, then iterate over all elements computing source indices for each output element. NumPy's fftshift uses np.roll() which itself uses concatenate of slices — also copies, but more cache-friendly. More importantly, for fftshift the result could be a view (via stride manipulation) for even-length axes, since it's just swapping two halves. At minimum, the current element-by-element index computation is O(n * ndim) per element; a block-copy approach (roll via slice concatenation) would be O(n) with better cache behavior.
